macro_summary: Markets enter a pivotal week with June CPI and Fed Chair Warsh's Congressional testimony both landing Tuesday July 14, creating a binary macro event that could reprice the current ~25% market-implied rate hike probability. FOMC meets July 28-29 with growth stocks particularly exposed to any hawkish re-rating. AI capex and memory supply narratives remain dominant sector themes, with TSMC's July 16 print serving as a near-term semiconductor read-through ahead of a cluster of mega-cap earnings in late July.
screening_note: 6 overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold to qualify was 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying tickers are included, ranked by appearance count descending.
key_events: July 13: SKHY permanent ticker officially begins US trading
July 14 8:30am ET: June CPI release — headline expected soft/negative MoM, core sticky ~2.9%
July 14: Fed Chair Warsh Congressional testimony — rate path commentary in focus
July 16: TSMC Q2 earnings — direct semiconductor sector read-through for NVDA/SKHY
July 28-29: FOMC rate decision (~25% hike probability currently priced)
July 29: Microsoft Q4 FY2026 earnings — Azure growth and AI capex guidance
July 30 after close: Apple Q3 FY2026 earnings
Late August (~Aug 26): NVIDIA Q2 FY2027 earnings

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 6 overnight screens — the highest consensus in this digest. The stock is down ~25% YTD despite 40% Azure YoY growth, trading at its cheapest forward multiple (~21-22x NTM) since 2023. With 55+ analysts averaging a ~$589 target vs. the current ~$383 price (a ~56% gap), a hard July 29 earnings catalyst provides a defined re-rating event. A call or call spread structure is the preferred expression to capture potential upside from an Azure guidance beat while managing binary risk around the same-day FOMC decision.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29, 2026 (coincides with FOMC decision day — dual binary risk/reward)
ideas.edge: ~56% gap to average analyst consensus target ($383 current vs. ~$589 avg target, 55+ analysts); cheapest forward multiple since 2023 at ~21-22x NTM despite 40% Azure growth
ideas.bull_case: Azure guidance beat on July 29 triggers analyst target upgrades and multiple re-expansion from historically cheap 21-22x; FOMC hold or dovish tone same day compounds the relief rally into a mega-cap compounder with durable AI revenue.
ideas.bear_case: FOMC surprises hawkish on July 29, compressing growth multiples on the same day as earnings; any slowdown in Azure growth or elevated AI capex commentary spooks the market and delays the re-rate thesis.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in all 6 overnight screens with the highest single-name conviction vote count (5 high). The stock trades at a forward P/E of ~21.7x versus its 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs), an extreme historical discount while data center revenue hit a record $75.2B last quarter (+85% YoY) and HBM supply remains sold out through most of 2027. TSMC's July 16 earnings are the near-term read-through catalyst; August 26 earnings are the hard event. A September call spread is the preferred structure, providing runway past both TSMC and NVDA's own print.
ideas.catalyst: TSMC earnings July 16 (sector read-through); NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 AMC
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of 21.7x vs. 5-year average of 72x (Goldman Sachs); 68 analysts with avg target ~$302.73 vs. ~$202 current price (~49% upside gap); HBM supply sold out through 2027
ideas.bull_case: TSMC beats on July 16 validating AI capex cycle; NVDA's own August 26 print confirms record demand; multiple re-expansion from deeply discounted 21.7x forward P/E toward historical norms drives a significant re-rate.
ideas.bear_case: Macro deterioration from hawkish CPI or FOMC repricing compresses semis broadly; any softness in hyperscaler AI capex guidance at MSFT/AAPL earnings reads through negatively before NVDA's own August print.

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 6 overnight screens and its permanent US ticker begins trading today (July 13), making this a real-time first-week price discovery opportunity. The IPO priced at $149, debuted at ~$168-170 (+13-14%), and was 7x oversubscribed — with no US-listed direct comparable at this scale. The CEO has publicly stated memory chip shortages could persist past 2030, and HBM supply to NVDA and AAPL is sold out through most of 2027. Revenue grew 46.76% in 2025 with earnings up 116.89%. All 6 screens flagged options as illiquid or unavailable on day-one — stock-only position sized moderately is the correct expression until options market matures.
ideas.catalyst: Permanent SKHY ticker officially live July 13, 2026 (today); Q2 2026 earnings expected next month; CEO reiterated memory shortage outlook past 2030
ideas.edge: IPO 7x oversubscribed; day-1 pop of ~13-14% from $149 IPO price; dominant global HBM supplier to NVDA and AAPL with no US-listed direct comp; global chip sales up 104% YoY in May 2026; HBM supply sold out through 2027+
ideas.bull_case: First-week institutional accumulation on permanent ticker combined with structural HBM supercycle demand drives sustained momentum; Q2 earnings next month provide a hard fundamental catalyst to sustain the premium valuation.
ideas.bear_case: Post-IPO lock-up expiry and early price discovery volatility create sharp pullbacks; options market immaturity means no defined-risk hedge is available; broader semis selloff on hawkish CPI or FOMC would disproportionately hit a newly-listed high-beta name.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 4 of 6 overnight screens exclusively as a macro hedge or binary-event trade vehicle. Tuesday July 14 brings both the June CPI print (8:30am ET) and Fed Chair Warsh's Congressional testimony — a dual macro binary with the current ~25% market-implied hike probability highly sensitive to the data. With the S&P near all-time highs (~7,500) and 61% market-implied odds of a September hike, an options straddle captures a move in either direction, while a put spread offers a cost-efficient portfolio hedge if headline CPI surprises hot.
ideas.catalyst: June CPI release July 14, 2026 at 8:30am ET + Fed Chair Warsh Congressional testimony same day; FOMC meeting July 28-29
ideas.edge: 25% market-implied July hike probability (61% September) creates asymmetric repricing risk on a hot print; QQQ historically shows 1-2% single-day moves on CPI surprises; short-dated options offer cost-efficient binary hedge with S&P near all-time highs amplifying tail magnitude
ideas.bull_case: Headline CPI prints soft or negative MoM (energy base effect dominates narrative), rate hike odds collapse, growth multiples expand and QQQ rallies 1-2%+ into the week — calls or a straddle capture this outcome.
ideas.bear_case: Core CPI remains sticky above 2.9% and Warsh signals hawkish intent; July hike probability reprices sharply higher, compressing Nasdaq-100 growth multiples on names already at elevated forward P/Es like AAPL (37x).

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens — the minimum threshold — but both appearances carried high conviction. The stock has pulled back ~9% over the past month to ~$284 against a consensus analyst target of ~$315 (~11% upside), creating a defined-risk entry into the July 30 earnings catalyst. Eight consecutive EPS beats, a $100B active buyback, Services revenue at an all-time high of ~$31B/quarter, and a fresh $30B Broadcom AI chip deal announced July 10 are the fundamental supports. An August expiry call spread is preferred over outright stock at a 37x P/E.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings July 30, 2026 after close; $30B Broadcom AI chip supply deal announced July 10, 2026
ideas.edge: ~11% gap to consensus analyst target ($284 current vs. ~$315); 8 consecutive EPS beats; $100B buyback active; Services at all-time high $30.98B/quarter; stock ~9% off recent highs into a hard earnings date
ideas.bull_case: 9th consecutive EPS beat on July 30 driven by Services all-time highs and AI feature upgrade cycle; Broadcom chip deal signals accelerating Apple Silicon roadmap; buyback provides floor and accelerates EPS growth.
ideas.bear_case: At 37x forward P/E, any miss or guidance cut is punished heavily; macro sensitivity to hawkish CPI repricing on July 14 and FOMC on July 28-29 could pressure the stock further before earnings even arrive; China revenue risk remains an ongoing overhang.
